tldr: Specialized gradient boosting ensemble (HistGB, XGBoost, CatBoost) with logistic regression meta-learner and isotonic calibration, trained via temporal walk-forward CV to predict NCAA tournament win probabilities while minimizing Brier Score.
summary: This notebook presents a specialized ensemble pipeline for the March Machine Learning Mania competition, aiming to minimize the Brier Score for NCAA tournament win probability prediction. It constructs a rich feature set at the team-season level, including Dean Oliver's Four Factors, Elo ratings with log-damped margin adjustments, Massey ordinal rankings, and historical seed upset probabilities, then converts them to matchup-level differences. To combat extreme model correlation, the feature space is strictly partitioned: XGBoost receives only on-court stats, CatBoost receives only external rankings, and HistGradientBoosting receives the full set. Out-of-fold predictions are transformed to log-odds and blended via an L2-regularized Logistic Regression meta-learner, followed by isotonic calibration. The pipeline uses strict temporal walk-forward cross-validation to prevent data leakage and parallelizes feature engineering and inference for efficiency.
extensive_summary:
## Data Loading & Feature Construction
The pipeline loads compact and detailed NCAA game results, Massey ordinal rankings (dynamically concatenating supplementary files), seeds, and team rosters for both Men's and Women's tournaments. All features are computed at the team-season level and converted to matchup-level differences (Team 1 minus Team 2). Baseline compact stats include WinRate, AvgMargin, and AvgScore. Detailed results unlock Dean Oliver's Four Factors + efficiency metrics: possessions are estimated using a 0.44 multiplier, yielding ORTG, DRTG, NetRtg, eFG%, TO%, ORB%, FTR, ThreePAr, and ASTpct. Additional signals include Strength of Schedule (avg opponent WinRate), rolling form (WinRate and AvgMargin over the final 10 games), and Massey ordinal rankings (aggregated as mean, median, std, best, worst rank, plus pivoted individual systems like POM, SAG, MOR, RTH, WLK, DOK, COL, AP). Elo ratings are updated game-by-game within each season with a +100 home-court adjustment, a log-damped margin-of-victory multiplier, and a 75/25 regression to the mean between seasons. Historical seed upset probabilities are built from past tournaments (min 5 observed seasons). Interaction features include `SeedDiff x EloDiff`, `SeedDiff²`, and `SeedDiff x NetRtg`.

## Validation & Hyperparameter Tuning
The notebook enforces strict temporal walk-forward cross-validation across seasons 2018 to 2025. For each validation season `v`, models train exclusively on `Season < v` and evaluate on `Season == v`, preventing future-data leakage. Hyperparameter optimization uses Optuna's TPE sampler with 40 trials on the 2025 held-out season, targeting Brier Score minimization for HistGradientBoosting. XGBoost and CatBoost use fixed defaults: 600 estimators, learning rate 0.03, and standard regularization/subsample parameters.

## Model Specialization & Stacking
To eliminate extreme inter-model correlation (>0.99), the feature space is strictly partitioned: XGBoost receives only on-court performance metrics (Four Factors, Elo, NetRtg, Margins), CatBoost receives only external rankings (Massey, KenPom, Sagarin), and HistGradientBoosting receives the full feature set. This drops correlation to ~0.83. Out-of-fold predictions are converted to log-odds to create a linear input space and blended via an L2-regularized Logistic Regression meta-learner (C=0.1) with StandardScaler. The `SeedDiff` feature is passed to the meta-learner to enable context-aware blending based on seed mismatch severity.

## Calibration & Inference
Blended OOF probabilities undergo isotonic regression calibration fitted strictly on OOF data to correct systematic over/under-confidence. Final training reuses the full dataset with specialized feature subsets. Inference parallelizes feature engineering across CPU cores and runs concurrent model predictions via `ThreadPoolExecutor`. Final predictions are clipped to [0.015, 0.985] before submission.
cv_strategy: Temporal walk-forward cross-validation (Season < v for train, Season == v for validation) across 2018-2025
preprocessing: Fill numeric NaNs with column medians
Convert base model probabilities to log-odds for meta-learner input
feature_engineering: Aggregations: team-season WinRate, AvgMargin, AvgScore, AvgOppScore; Massey rank stats (mean, median, std, best, worst)
Domain-derived: Dean Oliver's Four Factors + efficiency (ORTG, DRTG, NetRtg, eFG%, TO%, ORB%, FTR, ThreePAr, ASTpct) using 0.44 possession multiplier
Domain-derived: Strength of Schedule (avg opponent WinRate)
Time-based: Rolling form (WinRate & AvgMargin over last 10 games)
Domain-derived: Game-by-game Elo with home-court adjustment (+100), log-damped margin-of-victory multiplier, 75/25 season regression
Domain-derived: Historical seed upset probability lookup (min 5 seasons)
Interactions: Matchup-level differences (T1 - T2), SeedDiff x EloDiff, SeedDiff², SeedDiff x NetRtg
ensemble: Specialized feature partitioning across three tree models (XGBoost on stats, CatBoost on rankings, HistGB on all) to reduce correlation, followed by log-odds transformation and blending via an L2-regularized Logistic Regression meta-learner trained on OOF predictions.
post_processing: Isotonic regression calibration on OOF blended probabilities; final prediction clipping to [0.015, 0.985]
notable_techniques: Strict feature isolation to force ensemble diversity and reduce model correlation
Log-odds transformation of probabilities before meta-learner to create a more linear input space
Log-damped margin-of-victory multiplier in Elo updates to reward dominant wins proportionally
75/25 regression to the mean for Elo ratings between seasons
Using SeedDiff as a meta-feature to enable context-aware blending based on seed mismatch severity
Parallel feature engineering and concurrent model inference via ThreadPoolExecutor
Isotonic calibration fitted strictly on OOF predictions to prevent probability distribution tightening
